# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Jaro-Winkler for clap-style "Did you mean?"
# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def _jaro(s1, s2):
    if s1 == s2:
        return 1.0
    if not s1 or not s2:
        return 0.0
    win = max(len(s1), len(s2)) // 2 - 1
    if win < 0:
        win = 0
    m1 = [False] * len(s1)
    m2 = [False] * len(s2)
    matches = 0
    for i, c in enumerate(s1):
        a = max(0, i - win)
        b = min(i + win + 1, len(s2))
        for j in range(a, b):
            if m2[j] or s2[j] != c:
                continue
            m1[i] = True
            m2[j] = True
            matches += 1
            break
    if not matches:
        return 0.0
    t = 0
    k = 0
    for i, c in enumerate(s1):
        if not m1[i]:
            continue
        while not m2[k]:
            k += 1
        if c != s2[k]:
            t += 1
        k += 1
    t //= 2
    return (matches / len(s1) + matches / len(s2) + (matches - t) / matches) / 3


def jaro_winkler(s1, s2):
    j = _jaro(s1, s2)
    p = 0
    for c1, c2 in zip(s1, s2):
        if c1 == c2:
            p += 1
            if p == 4:
                break
        else:
            break
    return j + p * 0.1 * (1 - j)


def did_you_mean(arg, candidates):
    """clap's confidence-based suggestion. Threshold 0.8."""
    best = None
    best_score = 0.8
    for c in candidates:
        score = jaro_winkler(arg, c)
        if score > best_score:
            best_score = score
            best = c
    return best


# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# TOML — restricted parser/writer (only what pier needs)
# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TomlError(Exception):
    """Raised when TOML parsing fails. Mirrors toml-rs error messages."""
    def __init__(self, msg, line=None):
        super().__init__(msg)
        self.msg = msg
        self.line = line


_BARE_KEY_RE = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z0-9_\-]+")


class TomlParser:
    def __init__(self, text):
        # Normalize CRLF → LF for line counting.
        self.text = text
        self.pos = 0
        self.line = 1

    def _eof(self):
        return self.pos >= len(self.text)

    def _peek(self, n=1):
        return self.text[self.pos:self.pos + n]

    def _consume(self, n=1):
        out = self.text[self.pos:self.pos + n]
        for ch in out:
            if ch == "\n":
                self.line += 1
        self.pos += n
        return out

    def _skip_ws(self):
        while not self._eof() and self.text[self.pos] in " \t":
            self.pos += 1

    def _skip_blank_and_comments(self):
        # Skip whitespace, newlines, and # comments.
        while not self._eof():
            ch = self.text[self.pos]
            if ch in " \t":
                self.pos += 1
            elif ch == "\n":
                self.line += 1
                self.pos += 1
            elif ch == "\r":
                self.pos += 1
            elif ch == "#":
                while not self._eof() and self.text[self.pos] != "\n":
                    self.pos += 1
            else:
                break

    def parse(self):
        # Returns: {"scripts": {alias: {command, description?, tags?}}, "default": {...}, "_order": [aliases]}.
        result = {"scripts": {}, "default": {}, "_order": []}
        current_table = None  # ("scripts", alias) | ("default",) | None
        while not self._eof():
            self._skip_blank_and_comments()
            if self._eof():
                break
            if self.text[self.pos] == "[":
                # Table header.
                self.pos += 1
                self._skip_ws()
                key_parts = self._read_key_parts()
                self._skip_ws()
                if self._peek() != "]":
                    self._raise("expected a right bracket")
                self.pos += 1
                # Expect newline or eof.
                self._skip_ws()
                if not self._eof() and self.text[self.pos] not in ("\n", "\r", "#"):
                    self._raise("expected newline after table header")
                if key_parts == ["scripts"]:
                    # Bare [scripts] is unusual but tolerable as parent.
                    current_table = None
                elif len(key_parts) == 2 and key_parts[0] == "scripts":
                    alias = key_parts[1]
                    if alias not in result["scripts"]:
                        result["scripts"][alias] = {}
                        result["_order"].append(alias)
                    current_table = ("scripts", alias)
                elif key_parts == ["default"]:
                    current_table = ("default",)
                else:
                    # Unknown table — accept but ignore body.
                    current_table = ("__unknown__",)
            else:
                # key = value line.
                key_parts = self._read_key_parts()
                self._skip_ws()
                if self._peek() != "=":
                    self._raise("expected an equals")
                self.pos += 1
                self._skip_ws()
                value = self._read_value()
                # Assign to current table.
                if current_table is None:
                    pass
                elif current_table[0] == "scripts":
                    alias = current_table[1]
                    if len(key_parts) == 1:
                        k = key_parts[0]
                        if k == "command":
                            if not isinstance(value, str):
                                self._raise_type("string", value, f"scripts.{alias}.command")
                            result["scripts"][alias]["command"] = value
                        elif k == "description":
                            if not isinstance(value, str):
                                self._raise_type("string", value, f"scripts.{alias}.description")
                            result["scripts"][alias]["description"] = value
                        elif k == "tags":
                            if not isinstance(value, list):
                                self._raise_type("array", value, f"scripts.{alias}.tags")
                            for t in value:
                                if not isinstance(t, str):
                                    self._raise_type("string", t, f"scripts.{alias}.tags")
                            result["scripts"][alias]["tags"] = value
                        else:
                            pass  # silently ignore unknown keys
                elif current_table[0] == "default":
                    if len(key_parts) == 1:
                        k = key_parts[0]
                        if k == "command_width":
                            if isinstance(value, int):
                                result["default"]["command_width"] = value
                        # ignore other keys
                else:
                    pass  # unknown table, ignore key
        return result

    def _read_key_parts(self):
        parts = []
        while True:
            self._skip_ws()
            if self._eof():
                self._raise("expected a table key, found a right bracket")
            ch = self.text[self.pos]
            if ch == '"':
                # Quoted key.
                self.pos += 1
                s = []
                while not self._eof() and self.text[self.pos] != '"':
                    c = self.text[self.pos]
                    if c == "\\":
                        self.pos += 1
                        if self._eof():
                            self._raise("unterminated escape")
                        e = self.text[self.pos]
                        s.append(self._unescape(e))
                        self.pos += 1
                    else:
                        s.append(c)
                        self.pos += 1
                if self._eof():
                    self._raise("unterminated string key")
                self.pos += 1
                parts.append("".join(s))
            elif ch == "'":
                # Literal key.
                self.pos += 1
                start = self.pos
                while not self._eof() and self.text[self.pos] != "'":
                    self.pos += 1
                if self._eof():
                    self._raise("unterminated literal key")
                parts.append(self.text[start:self.pos])
                self.pos += 1
            else:
                m = _BARE_KEY_RE.match(self.text, self.pos)
                if not m:
                    self._raise("expected a table key, found a right bracket")
                parts.append(m.group(0))
                self.pos = m.end()
            self._skip_ws()
            if not self._eof() and self.text[self.pos] == ".":
                self.pos += 1
                continue
            break
        return parts

    def _read_value(self):
        if self._eof():
            self._raise("expected a value")
        ch = self.text[self.pos]
        if ch == "'":
            return self._read_literal_string()
        if ch == '"':
            return self._read_basic_string()
        if ch == "[":
            return self._read_array()
        if ch == "{":
            return self._read_inline_table()
        if ch in "0123456789-+":
            return self._read_number_or_bool()
        if ch in "tf":
            return self._read_bool_or_id()
        self._raise(f"unexpected character {ch!r}")

    def _read_literal_string(self):
        # Triple-single or single.
        if self.text[self.pos:self.pos + 3] == "'''":
            self.pos += 3
            # Optional leading newline.
            if not self._eof() and self.text[self.pos] == "\n":
                self.pos += 1
                self.line += 1
            elif self.text[self.pos:self.pos + 2] == "\r\n":
                self.pos += 2
                self.line += 1
            start = self.pos
            while True:
                if self._eof():
                    self._raise("unterminated literal block string")
                if self.text[self.pos:self.pos + 3] == "'''":
                    s = self.text[start:self.pos]
                    self.pos += 3
                    return s
                if self.text[self.pos] == "\n":
                    self.line += 1
                self.pos += 1
        else:
            self.pos += 1
            start = self.pos
            while True:
                if self._eof():
                    self._raise("unterminated literal string")
                c = self.text[self.pos]
                if c == "'":
                    s = self.text[start:self.pos]
                    self.pos += 1
                    return s
                if c == "\n":
                    self._raise("newline in literal string")
                self.pos += 1

    def _read_basic_string(self):
        # Triple-double or single.
        if self.text[self.pos:self.pos + 3] == '"""':
            self.pos += 3
            if not self._eof() and self.text[self.pos] == "\n":
                self.pos += 1
                self.line += 1
            elif self.text[self.pos:self.pos + 2] == "\r\n":
                self.pos += 2
                self.line += 1
            out = []
            while True:
                if self._eof():
                    self._raise("unterminated basic block string")
                if self.text[self.pos:self.pos + 3] == '"""':
                    self.pos += 3
                    return "".join(out)
                c = self.text[self.pos]
                if c == "\\":
                    self.pos += 1
                    if self._eof():
                        self._raise("unterminated escape")
                    nxt = self.text[self.pos]
                    if nxt == "\n":
                        # Line ending escape: skip whitespace.
                        self.pos += 1
                        self.line += 1
                        while not self._eof() and self.text[self.pos] in " \t\n\r":
                            if self.text[self.pos] == "\n":
                                self.line += 1
                            self.pos += 1
                    else:
                        out.append(self._unescape(nxt))
                        self.pos += 1
                else:
                    if c == "\n":
                        self.line += 1
                    out.append(c)
                    self.pos += 1
        else:
            self.pos += 1
            out = []
            while True:
                if self._eof():
                    self._raise("unterminated basic string")
                c = self.text[self.pos]
                if c == '"':
                    self.pos += 1
                    return "".join(out)
                if c == "\\":
                    self.pos += 1
                    if self._eof():
                        self._raise("unterminated escape")
                    nxt = self.text[self.pos]
                    out.append(self._unescape(nxt))
                    self.pos += 1
                elif c == "\n":
                    self._raise("newline in basic string")
                else:
                    out.append(c)
                    self.pos += 1

    def _unescape(self, c):
        return {
            "b": "\b", "t": "\t", "n": "\n", "f": "\f",
            "r": "\r", '"': '"', "\\": "\\", "/": "/",
        }.get(c, c)

    def _read_array(self):
        self.pos += 1
        items = []
        while True:
            self._skip_array_ws()
            if self._eof():
                self._raise("unterminated array")
            if self.text[self.pos] == "]":
                self.pos += 1
                return items
            items.append(self._read_value())
            self._skip_array_ws()
            if self._eof():
                self._raise("unterminated array")
            if self.text[self.pos] == ",":
                self.pos += 1
                continue
            if self.text[self.pos] == "]":
                self.pos += 1
                return items
            self._raise("expected comma or close bracket in array")

    def _skip_array_ws(self):
        while not self._eof():
            ch = self.text[self.pos]
            if ch in " \t\r":
                self.pos += 1
            elif ch == "\n":
                self.line += 1
                self.pos += 1
            elif ch == "#":
                while not self._eof() and self.text[self.pos] != "\n":
                    self.pos += 1
            else:
                break

    def _read_inline_table(self):
        self.pos += 1
        result = {}
        while True:
            self._skip_ws()
            if self._eof():
                self._raise("unterminated inline table")
            if self.text[self.pos] == "}":
                self.pos += 1
                return result
            kp = self._read_key_parts()
            self._skip_ws()
            if self._peek() != "=":
                self._raise("expected =")
            self.pos += 1
            self._skip_ws()
            v = self._read_value()
            d = result
            for p in kp[:-1]:
                d = d.setdefault(p, {})
            d[kp[-1]] = v
            self._skip_ws()
            if self._peek() == ",":
                self.pos += 1
                continue
            if self._peek() == "}":
                self.pos += 1
                return result

    def _read_number_or_bool(self):
        start = self.pos
        if self.text[self.pos] in "+-":
            self.pos += 1
        while not self._eof() and self.text[self.pos] in "0123456789_":
            self.pos += 1
        # No floats needed for pier's schema.
        s = self.text[start:self.pos].replace("_", "")
        try:
            return int(s)
        except ValueError:
            self._raise("invalid number")

    def _read_bool_or_id(self):
        if self.text[self.pos:self.pos + 4] == "true":
            self.pos += 4
            return True
        if self.text[self.pos:self.pos + 5] == "false":
            self.pos += 5
            return False
        # Unknown identifier — error.
        m = _BARE_KEY_RE.match(self.text, self.pos)
        if m:
            self.pos = m.end()
            self._raise("invalid value")
        self._raise("invalid value")

    def _raise(self, msg):
        raise TomlError(msg, self.line)

    def _raise_type(self, expected, got, key):
        if isinstance(got, int):
            got_repr = f"integer `{got}`"
            kind = "integer"
        elif isinstance(got, str):
            got_repr = f"string `\"{got}\"`"
            kind = "string"
        elif isinstance(got, list):
            kind = "array"
            got_repr = "array"
        elif isinstance(got, dict):
            kind = "map"
            got_repr = "map"
        else:
            kind = "value"
            got_repr = str(got)
        raise TomlError(f"invalid type: {got_repr}, expected a {expected} for key `{key}`", self.line)


def parse_toml(text):
    return TomlParser(text).parse()


# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# TOML writer — produces output matching pier's exact format
# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

_BARE_KEY_FULL_RE = re.compile(r"\A[A-Za-z0-9_\-]+\Z")


def _format_key(k):
    if k and _BARE_KEY_FULL_RE.match(k):
        return k
    # Quoted key with escapes.
    s = []
    for c in k:
        if c == "\\":
            s.append("\\\\")
        elif c == '"':
            s.append('\\"')
        elif c == "\b":
            s.append("\\b")
        elif c == "\t":
            s.append("\\t")
        elif c == "\n":
            s.append("\\n")
        elif c == "\f":
            s.append("\\f")
        elif c == "\r":
            s.append("\\r")
        elif ord(c) < 0x20:
            s.append("\\u%04X" % ord(c))
        else:
            s.append(c)
    return '"' + "".join(s) + '"'


def _format_string(s):
    """Match pier's serializer (toml-rs):
    - if contains '\n' → use '''...''' literal block (with leading newline)
    - else if contains '\'' but no '"' → use "..." basic
    - else if contains '\'' and '"' → use '''...''' literal block (no leading newline)
    - else → use '...' literal
    """
    has_nl = "\n" in s
    has_sq = "'" in s
    has_dq = '"' in s
    if has_nl:
        # Triple-single literal with leading newline (pier's style).
        return "'''\n" + s + "'''"
    if has_sq and not has_dq:
        # Use basic string.
        out = []
        for c in s:
            if c == "\\":
                out.append("\\\\")
            elif c == '"':
                out.append('\\"')
            elif c == "\b":
                out.append("\\b")
            elif c == "\t":
                out.append("\\t")
            elif c == "\f":
                out.append("\\f")
            elif c == "\r":
                out.append("\\r")
            elif ord(c) < 0x20 or ord(c) == 0x7F:
                out.append("\\u%04X" % ord(c))
            else:
                out.append(c)
        return '"' + "".join(out) + '"'
    if has_sq and has_dq:
        # Use triple-single literal (no leading newline).
        return "'''" + s + "'''"
    # Simple literal.
    return "'" + s + "'"


def write_config(parsed):
    """Serialize parsed config back to TOML in pier's format.
    Sections are: scripts.* sorted alphabetically, then [default]."""
    lines = []
    aliases = sorted(parsed["scripts"].keys())
    for alias in aliases:
        s = parsed["scripts"][alias]
        lines.append(f"[scripts.{_format_key(alias)}]")
        # Keys in fixed order: command, description, tags
        if "command" in s:
            lines.append(f"command = {_format_string(s['command'])}")
        if "description" in s:
            lines.append(f"description = {_format_string(s['description'])}")
        if "tags" in s:
            tags = s["tags"]
            if not tags:
                lines.append("tags = []")
            else:
                lines.append("tags = [")
                for t in tags:
                    lines.append(f"    {_format_string(t)},")
                lines.append("]")
        lines.append("")
    lines.append("[default]")
    d = parsed["default"]
    if "command_width" in d:
        lines.append(f"command_width = {d['command_width']}")
    return "\n".join(lines) + "\n"


# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Config path resolution
# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def get_config_path(explicit=None):
    """Find the config file. Returns (path, error_or_None).
    For commands that NEED an existing file, error if none found.
    """
    if explicit:
        return explicit, None
    env_path = os.environ.get("PIER_CONFIG_PATH")
    if env_path:
        return env_path, None
    candidates = []
    candidates.append(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "pier.toml"))
    xdg = os.environ.get("XDG_CONFIG_HOME") or ""
    home = os.environ.get("HOME") or ""
    if not xdg and home:
        xdg = os.path.join(home, ".config")
    if xdg:
        candidates.append(os.path.join(xdg, "pier", "config.toml"))
        candidates.append(os.path.join(xdg, "pier", "config"))
        candidates.append(os.path.join(xdg, "pier.toml"))
    if home:
        candidates.append(os.path.join(home, ".pier.toml"))
        candidates.append(os.path.join(home, ".pier"))
    for p in candidates:
        if os.path.isfile(p):
            return p, None
    return None, "No default config file found. See help for more info."


def get_init_path(explicit=None):
    """For config-init: where to create the file."""
    if explicit:
        return explicit
    env_path = os.environ.get("PIER_CONFIG_PATH")
    if env_path:
        return env_path
    xdg = os.environ.get("XDG_CONFIG_HOME") or ""
    home = os.environ.get("HOME") or ""
    if not xdg and home:
        xdg = os.path.join(home, ".config")
    if xdg:
        return os.path.join(xdg, "pier", "config.toml")
    if home:
        return os.path.join(home, ".pier.toml")
    return None


# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Helpers
# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def err(msg, exit_code=1):
    sys.stderr.write(f"error: {msg}\n")
    sys.exit(exit_code)


def out(msg, end="\n"):
    sys.stdout.write(msg + end)


def load_config(path):
    if not os.path.exists(path):
        err(f"Unable to read config from file {path}: No such file or directory (os error 2) ")
    try:
        with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            text = f.read()
    except OSError as e:
        err(f"Unable to read config from file {path}: {e.strerror} (os error {e.errno}) ")
    try:
        return parse_toml(text)
    except TomlError as e:
        msg = e.msg
        if e.line is not None:
            msg = f"{msg} at line {e.line}"
        err(f"Unable to parse toml config from file {path}: {msg}")


def save_config(path, parsed):
    text = write_config(parsed)
    with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        f.write(text)


def require_scripts(parsed):
    if not parsed["scripts"]:
        err("No scripts exist. Would you like to add a new script?")


# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Subcommand handlers
# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

LIST_HORIZ = "≖"  # ≖
LIST_VERT = "⋮"   # ⋮


def cmd_list(parsed, opts):
    require_scripts(parsed)
    scripts = parsed["scripts"]
    cmd_full = opts.get("cmd_full", False)
    cmd_width = opts.get("cmd_width")  # cli option
    list_aliases = opts.get("list_aliases", False)
    tags_filter = opts.get("tags", [])

    # Filter; if multiple tags, include alias once per matching tag.
    keys = sorted(scripts.keys())
    if tags_filter:
        rows = []
        for tag in tags_filter:
            for k in keys:
                stags = scripts[k].get("tags") or []
                if tag in stags:
                    rows.append(k)
    else:
        rows = list(keys)

    if list_aliases:
        for k in rows:
            out(k)
        return

    # Determine command truncation width.
    if cmd_full:
        max_cmd = None
    elif cmd_width is not None:
        max_cmd = cmd_width
    else:
        # Look at [default] config option.
        dw = parsed["default"].get("command_width")
        max_cmd = dw if dw is not None else 80

    def truncate(s):
        if max_cmd is None:
            return s
        return s[:max_cmd]

    headers = ["Alias", "Tags", "Command", "Description"]
    data = []
    for alias in rows:
        s = scripts[alias]
        cmd = truncate(s.get("command", ""))
        desc = s.get("description", "")
        tags = ",".join(s.get("tags") or [])
        data.append([alias, tags, cmd, desc])

    # Compute column widths.
    widths = [len(h) for h in headers]
    for row in data:
        for i, cell in enumerate(row):
            if len(cell) > widths[i]:
                widths[i] = len(cell)

    # Render table.
    total = sum(widths) + 2 * len(widths) + (len(widths) + 1)
    bar = LIST_HORIZ * total
    out(bar)
    header_line = LIST_VERT + " " + " ".join(
        h.ljust(widths[i]) + " " + LIST_VERT for i, h in enumerate(headers)
    )
    # Adjust header_line so it has " VERT " between cells, ending with VERT.
    parts = []
    for i, h in enumerate(headers):
        parts.append(" " + h.ljust(widths[i]) + " ")
    out(LIST_VERT + LIST_VERT.join(parts) + LIST_VERT)
    out(bar)
    for row in data:
        parts = []
        for i, cell in enumerate(row):
            parts.append(" " + cell.ljust(widths[i]) + " ")
        out(LIST_VERT + LIST_VERT.join(parts) + LIST_VERT)
    out(bar)


def cmd_add(parsed, opts, path):
    alias = opts["alias"]
    force = opts.get("force", False)
    command = opts.get("command")
    description = opts.get("description")
    tags = opts.get("tags")

    if alias in parsed["scripts"] and not force:
        err(f"AliasAlreadyExists:  {alias}")

    if command is None:
        # Open editor.
        command = open_editor("")

    entry = {"command": command}
    if description is not None:
        entry["description"] = description
    if tags:
        entry["tags"] = list(tags)
    parsed["scripts"][alias] = entry
    save_config(path, parsed)
    out(f"Added {alias}")


def cmd_remove(parsed, opts, path):
    require_scripts(parsed)
    alias = opts["alias"]
    if alias not in parsed["scripts"]:
        err(f"AliasNotFound: No script found by alias {alias}")
    del parsed["scripts"][alias]
    save_config(path, parsed)
    out(f"Removed {alias}")


def cmd_show(parsed, opts):
    require_scripts(parsed)
    alias = opts["alias"]
    if alias not in parsed["scripts"]:
        err(f"AliasNotFound: No script found by alias {alias}")
    out(parsed["scripts"][alias]["command"])


def cmd_edit(parsed, opts, path):
    require_scripts(parsed)
    alias = opts["alias"]
    if alias not in parsed["scripts"]:
        err(f"AliasNotFound: No script found by alias {alias}")
    current = parsed["scripts"][alias]["command"]
    new_cmd = open_editor(current)
    parsed["scripts"][alias]["command"] = new_cmd
    save_config(path, parsed)
    out(f"Edited {alias}")


def cmd_run(parsed, opts, verbose):
    require_scripts(parsed)
    alias = opts["alias"]
    args = opts.get("args", [])
    if alias not in parsed["scripts"]:
        err(f"AliasNotFound: No script found by alias {alias}")
    command = parsed["scripts"][alias]["command"]
    shell = os.environ.get("SHELL", "/bin/sh")
    if verbose:
        out(f'Starting script "{alias}"')
        out("-" * 25)
        sys.stdout.flush()
    try:
        rc = subprocess.call([shell, "-c", command, alias] + args)
    except OSError as e:
        err(f"Command execution failed with: {e.strerror} (os error {e.errno})")
    if verbose:
        sys.stdout.flush()
        out("-" * 25)
        out("Script complete")
    sys.exit(rc)


def cmd_move(parsed, opts, path):
    require_scripts(parsed)
    src = opts["from"]
    dst = opts["to"]
    force = opts.get("force", False)
    if src not in parsed["scripts"]:
        err(f"AliasNotFound: No script found by alias {src}")
    if dst in parsed["scripts"] and not force:
        err(f"AliasAlreadyExists:  {dst}")
    parsed["scripts"][dst] = parsed["scripts"][src]
    if src != dst:
        del parsed["scripts"][src]
    save_config(path, parsed)
    out(f"Move from alias {src} to new alias {dst}")


def cmd_copy(parsed, opts, path):
    require_scripts(parsed)
    src = opts["from"]
    dst = opts["to"]
    if src not in parsed["scripts"]:
        err(f"AliasNotFound: No script found by alias {src}")
    if dst in parsed["scripts"]:
        err(f"AliasAlreadyExists:  {dst}")
    import copy as _copymod
    parsed["scripts"][dst] = _copymod.deepcopy(parsed["scripts"][src])
    save_config(path, parsed)
    out(f"Copy from alias {src} to new alias {dst}")


def cmd_config_init(path):
    if path is None:
        err("No default config file found. See help for more info.")
    if os.path.exists(path):
        err(f"Cannot initialize config at {path}, file already exists.")
    parent = os.path.dirname(path)
    if parent and not os.path.isdir(parent):
        try:
            os.mkdir(parent)
        except OSError as e:
            err(f"Failed to create directory. {e.strerror} (os error {e.errno})")
    parsed = {
        "scripts": {
            "hello-pier": {
                "command": "echo Hello, Pier!",
                "description": "This is an example command.",
            }
        },
        "default": {},
        "_order": ["hello-pier"],
    }
    save_config(path, parsed)
    out("Added hello-pier")


def open_editor(initial=""):
    editor = os.environ.get("EDITOR") or "vi"
    fd, tmpfile = tempfile.mkstemp(prefix="xvrqt_scrawl_")
    try:
        with os.fdopen(fd, "w") as f:
            f.write(initial)
        try:
            rc = subprocess.call([editor, tmpfile])
        except OSError:
            err(f"EditorError: Failed when trying to get input from editor Failed to open `{editor}` as a text editor or editor was terminated with errors.")
        if rc != 0:
            err(f"EditorError: Failed when trying to get input from editor Failed to open `{editor}` as a text editor or editor was terminated with errors.")
        with open(tmpfile, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            return f.read()
    finally:
        try:
            os.unlink(tmpfile)
        except OSError:
            pass


# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# CLI parsing
# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def usage_main():
    return (
        "USAGE:\n"
        "    executable [FLAGS] [OPTIONS] <alias> [args]...\n"
        "    executable [FLAGS] [OPTIONS] <SUBCOMMAND>\n"
        "\n"
        "For more information try --help"
    )


def usage_alias_verbose():
    return "USAGE:\n    executable <alias> --verbose\n\nFor more information try --help"


def usage_sub(name):
    return SUB_HELP[name]["long"].split("USAGE:\n", 1)[1].split("\n\n", 1)[0]


def emit_clap_error(message, usage_block):
    """Emit a clap-style error to stderr: 'error: MSG\n\nUSAGE: ...\n\nFor more information try --help\n'"""
    sys.stderr.write(f"error: {message}\n\n")
    sys.stderr.write(usage_block)
    sys.stderr.write("\nFor more information try --help\n")
    sys.exit(1)


def emit_sub_clap_error(message, sub_name):
    block = "USAGE:\n    " + usage_sub_short(sub_name) + "\n"
    sys.stderr.write(f"error: {message}\n\n")
    sys.stderr.write(block)
    sys.stderr.write("\nFor more information try --help\n")
    sys.exit(1)


def usage_sub_short(name):
    """Just the USAGE line for a subcommand."""
    return {
        "add": "executable add [FLAGS] [OPTIONS] --alias <alias> [--] [command]",
        "remove": "executable remove <alias>",
        "list": "executable list [FLAGS] [OPTIONS]",
        "run": "executable run <alias> [args]...",
        "show": "executable show <alias>",
        "edit": "executable edit <alias>",
        "move": "executable move [FLAGS] <from-alias> <to-alias>",
        "copy": "executable copy <from-alias> <to-alias>",
        "config-init": "executable config-init",
        "help": "executable help <subcommands>...",
    }[name]


def main(argv):
    # Initial scan for global flags.
    verbose = False
    config_file = None
    i = 0
    positional = []
    show_help = False
    show_version = False
    while i < len(argv):
        a = argv[i]
        if a == "--":
            i += 1
            while i < len(argv):
                positional.append(argv[i])
                i += 1
            break
        if a == "-h" or a == "--help":
            show_help = True
            i += 1
            continue
        if a == "-V" or a == "--version":
            show_version = True
            i += 1
            continue
        if a == "-v" or a == "--verbose":
            if verbose:
                emit_clap_error(
                    "The argument '--verbose' was provided more than once, but cannot be used multiple times",
                    usage_alias_verbose(),
                )
            verbose = True
            i += 1
            continue
        if a == "-c" or a == "--config-file":
            if i + 1 >= len(argv):
                emit_clap_error(
                    "The argument '--config-file <path>' requires a value but none was supplied",
                    usage_main_alias(),
                )
            config_file = argv[i + 1]
            i += 2
            continue
        if a.startswith("--config-file="):
            config_file = a.split("=", 1)[1]
            i += 1
            continue
        if a.startswith("-c="):
            config_file = a.split("=", 1)[1]
            i += 1
            continue
        if a.startswith("--") or (len(a) > 1 and a.startswith("-") and not a[1].isdigit()):
            # Unknown flag at top level.
            emit_clap_error(
                f"Found argument '{a}' which wasn't expected, or isn't valid in this context",
                usage_main_alias(),
            )
        # First non-flag positional.
        positional.append(a)
        i += 1
        # The rest of args: read them as positional.
        while i < len(argv):
            positional.append(argv[i])
            i += 1
        break

    if show_help:
        sys.stdout.write(HELP_LONG)
        sys.exit(0)
    if show_version:
        sys.stdout.write(f"pier {VERSION}\n")
        sys.exit(0)

    if not positional:
        emit_clap_error(
            "The following required arguments were not provided:\n    <alias>",
            usage_main_alias(),
        )

    first = positional[0]
    rest = positional[1:]

    # Check if first is a subcommand (exact).
    sub = None
    if first in SUBCOMMANDS:
        sub = first
    elif first in SUB_ALIASES:
        sub = SUB_ALIASES[first]
    if sub:
        dispatch_sub(sub, rest, config_file, verbose)
        return

    # Otherwise it could be alias (implicit run).
    # But first check if it's *close to* a subcommand → clap suggestion.
    suggestion = did_you_mean(first, sorted(ALL_NAMES))
    if suggestion is not None:
        emit_subcommand_unrecognized(first, suggestion)

    # Treat as alias for implicit run.
    dispatch_implicit_run(first, rest, config_file, verbose)


def usage_main_alias():
    return (
        "USAGE:\n"
        "    executable [FLAGS] [OPTIONS] <alias> [args]...\n"
        "    executable [FLAGS] [OPTIONS] <SUBCOMMAND>"
    )


def emit_subcommand_unrecognized(arg, suggestion=None):
    msg = f"error: The subcommand '{arg}' wasn't recognized\n"
    if suggestion:
        msg += f"\tDid you mean '{suggestion}'?\n"
    msg += f"\nIf you believe you received this message in error, try re-running with 'executable -- {arg}'\n\n"
    msg += usage_main_alias() + "\n\nFor more information try --help\n"
    sys.stderr.write(msg)
    sys.exit(1)


def dispatch_implicit_run(alias, args, config_file, verbose):
    # When invoked as `executable <alias> [args]...`, any arg that's a subcommand
    # prefix triggers clap's "did you mean" error. Mirror that behavior.
    for a in args:
        if a.startswith("-") and not a == "--":
            # Unknown flag in this position.
            emit_clap_error(
                f"Found argument '{a}' which wasn't expected, or isn't valid in this context",
                usage_main_alias(),
            )
        suggestion = did_you_mean(a, sorted(ALL_NAMES))
        if suggestion is not None:
            emit_subcommand_unrecognized(a, suggestion)
    path, error = get_config_path(config_file)
    if error:
        err(error)
    parsed = load_config(path)
    cmd_run(parsed, {"alias": alias, "args": args}, verbose)


def dispatch_sub(sub, rest, config_file, verbose):
    # Subcommand-specific help / version.
    # Strip out -h/-V global recheck inside subcommand.
    if sub == "help":
        handle_help(rest)
        return
    parsed_opts = parse_sub_args(sub, rest)
    if parsed_opts.get("__help"):
        sys.stdout.write(SUB_HELP[sub]["long"])
        sys.exit(0)
    if parsed_opts.get("__version"):
        sys.stdout.write(SUB_HELP[sub]["version"])
        sys.exit(0)

    # Subcommands needing config file:
    if sub == "config-init":
        path = get_init_path(config_file)
        cmd_config_init(path)
        return

    path, error = get_config_path(config_file)
    if error:
        err(error)
    parsed = load_config(path)
    if sub == "list":
        cmd_list(parsed, parsed_opts)
    elif sub == "add":
        cmd_add(parsed, parsed_opts, path)
    elif sub == "remove":
        cmd_remove(parsed, parsed_opts, path)
    elif sub == "show":
        cmd_show(parsed, parsed_opts)
    elif sub == "edit":
        cmd_edit(parsed, parsed_opts, path)
    elif sub == "run":
        cmd_run(parsed, parsed_opts, verbose)
    elif sub == "move":
        cmd_move(parsed, parsed_opts, path)
    elif sub == "copy":
        cmd_copy(parsed, parsed_opts, path)


def handle_help(rest):
    # `executable help` → top-level help (long).
    if not rest:
        sys.stdout.write(HELP_LONG)
        sys.exit(0)
    # `executable help SUB`
    name = rest[0]
    canonical = name if name in SUBCOMMANDS else SUB_ALIASES.get(name)
    if canonical is None or canonical not in SUB_HELP:
        sys.stderr.write(
            f"error: The subcommand '{name}' wasn't recognized\n\n"
            "USAGE:\n\texecutable help <subcommands>...\n\n"
            "For more information try --help\n"
        )
        sys.exit(1)
    sys.stdout.write(SUB_HELP[canonical]["long"])
    sys.exit(0)


def parse_sub_args(sub, args):
    """Parse args for a specific subcommand. Returns dict of options/positionals."""
    opts = {}
    i = 0
    pos = []
    end_of_options = False
    while i < len(args):
        a = args[i]
        if end_of_options:
            pos.append(a)
            i += 1
            continue
        if a == "--":
            end_of_options = True
            i += 1
            continue
        if a == "-h" or a == "--help":
            opts["__help"] = True
            i += 1
            continue
        if a == "-V" or a == "--version":
            opts["__version"] = True
            i += 1
            continue
        # Subcommand-specific.
        if sub == "add":
            if a == "-f" or a == "--force":
                opts["force"] = True
                i += 1
                continue
            if a == "-a" or a == "--alias":
                if i + 1 >= len(args):
                    emit_clap_error(
                        "The argument '--alias <alias>' requires a value but none was supplied",
                        "USAGE:\n    executable add [FLAGS] [OPTIONS] --alias <alias> [--] [command]",
                    )
                opts["alias"] = args[i + 1]
                i += 2
                continue
            if a.startswith("--alias="):
                opts["alias"] = a.split("=", 1)[1]
                i += 1
                continue
            if a == "-d" or a == "--description":
                if i + 1 >= len(args):
                    emit_clap_error(
                        "The argument '--description <description>' requires a value but none was supplied",
                        "USAGE:\n    executable add [FLAGS] [OPTIONS] --alias <alias> [--] [command]",
                    )
                opts["description"] = args[i + 1]
                i += 2
                continue
            if a.startswith("--description="):
                opts["description"] = a.split("=", 1)[1]
                i += 1
                continue
            if a == "-t" or a == "--tag":
                # Multi-value: consume until next flag.
                tags = opts.setdefault("tags", [])
                i += 1
                if i >= len(args):
                    emit_clap_error(
                        "The argument '--tag <tags>...' requires a value but none was supplied",
                        "USAGE:\n    executable add [FLAGS] [OPTIONS] --alias <alias> [--] [command]",
                    )
                while i < len(args) and not args[i].startswith("-") and args[i] != "--":
                    tags.append(args[i])
                    i += 1
                continue
            if a.startswith("-"):
                emit_clap_error(
                    f"Found argument '{a}' which wasn't expected, or isn't valid in this context",
                    "USAGE:\n    executable add [FLAGS] [OPTIONS] --alias <alias> [--] [command]",
                )
            pos.append(a)
            i += 1
            continue
        if sub == "list":
            if a == "-l" or a == "--cmd_full":
                opts["cmd_full"] = True
                i += 1
                continue
            if a == "-q" or a == "--list_aliases":
                opts["list_aliases"] = True
                i += 1
                continue
            if a == "-c" or a == "--cmd_width":
                if i + 1 >= len(args):
                    emit_clap_error(
                        "The argument '--cmd_width <cmd-width>' requires a value but none was supplied",
                        "USAGE:\n    executable list --cmd_width <cmd-width>",
                    )
                val = args[i + 1]
                try:
                    opts["cmd_width"] = int(val)
                except ValueError:
                    sys.stderr.write(
                        f"error: Invalid value for '--cmd_width <cmd-width>': invalid digit found in string\n"
                    )
                    sys.exit(1)
                i += 2
                continue
            if a.startswith("--cmd_width="):
                try:
                    opts["cmd_width"] = int(a.split("=", 1)[1])
                except ValueError:
                    sys.stderr.write(
                        f"error: Invalid value for '--cmd_width <cmd-width>': invalid digit found in string\n"
                    )
                    sys.exit(1)
                i += 1
                continue
            if a == "-t" or a == "--tag":
                tags = opts.setdefault("tags", [])
                i += 1
                if i >= len(args):
                    emit_clap_error(
                        "The argument '--tag <tags>...' requires a value but none was supplied",
                        "USAGE:\n    executable list --tag <tags>...",
                    )
                while i < len(args) and not args[i].startswith("-") and args[i] != "--":
                    tags.append(args[i])
                    i += 1
                continue
            if a.startswith("-"):
                emit_clap_error(
                    f"Found argument '{a}' which wasn't expected, or isn't valid in this context",
                    "USAGE:\n    executable list [FLAGS] [OPTIONS]",
                )
            pos.append(a)
            i += 1
            continue
        if sub in ("remove", "show", "edit"):
            if a.startswith("-"):
                emit_clap_error(
                    f"Found argument '{a}' which wasn't expected, or isn't valid in this context",
                    f"USAGE:\n    executable {sub} <alias>",
                )
            pos.append(a)
            i += 1
            continue
        if sub == "run":
            if a.startswith("-") and len(pos) == 0:
                emit_clap_error(
                    f"Found argument '{a}' which wasn't expected, or isn't valid in this context",
                    "USAGE:\n    executable run <alias> [args]...",
                )
            pos.append(a)
            i += 1
            # Remaining args are all positional (script args).
            while i < len(args):
                if args[i] == "--":
                    i += 1
                    continue
                pos.append(args[i])
                i += 1
            continue
        if sub in ("move", "copy"):
            if a == "-f" or a == "--force":
                if sub == "copy":
                    emit_clap_error(
                        f"Found argument '{a}' which wasn't expected, or isn't valid in this context",
                        f"USAGE:\n    executable copy <from-alias> <to-alias>",
                    )
                opts["force"] = True
                i += 1
                continue
            if a.startswith("-"):
                emit_clap_error(
                    f"Found argument '{a}' which wasn't expected, or isn't valid in this context",
                    f"USAGE:\n    executable {sub} [FLAGS] <from-alias> <to-alias>" if sub == "move"
                    else f"USAGE:\n    executable copy <from-alias> <to-alias>",
                )
            pos.append(a)
            i += 1
            continue
        if sub == "config-init":
            if a.startswith("-"):
                emit_clap_error(
                    f"Found argument '{a}' which wasn't expected, or isn't valid in this context",
                    "USAGE:\n    executable config-init",
                )
            pos.append(a)
            i += 1
            continue
        # Default fallback
        pos.append(a)
        i += 1

    # Validate required positionals + finalize.
    if opts.get("__help") or opts.get("__version"):
        return opts

    if sub == "add":
        if "alias" not in opts:
            emit_clap_error(
                "The following required arguments were not provided:\n    --alias <alias>",
                "USAGE:\n    executable add [FLAGS] [OPTIONS] --alias <alias> [--] [command]",
            )
        if len(pos) > 1:
            emit_clap_error(
                f"Found argument '{pos[1]}' which wasn't expected, or isn't valid in this context",
                "USAGE:\n    executable add [FLAGS] [OPTIONS] --alias <alias> [--] [command]",
            )
        if pos:
            opts["command"] = pos[0]
        return opts
    if sub == "remove":
        if not pos:
            emit_clap_error(
                "The following required arguments were not provided:\n    <alias>",
                "USAGE:\n    executable remove <alias>",
            )
        if len(pos) > 1:
            emit_clap_error(
                f"Found argument '{pos[1]}' which wasn't expected, or isn't valid in this context",
                "USAGE:\n    executable remove <alias>",
            )
        opts["alias"] = pos[0]
        return opts
    if sub == "show":
        if not pos:
            emit_clap_error(
                "The following required arguments were not provided:\n    <alias>",
                "USAGE:\n    executable show <alias>",
            )
        if len(pos) > 1:
            emit_clap_error(
                f"Found argument '{pos[1]}' which wasn't expected, or isn't valid in this context",
                "USAGE:\n    executable show <alias>",
            )
        opts["alias"] = pos[0]
        return opts
    if sub == "edit":
        if not pos:
            emit_clap_error(
                "The following required arguments were not provided:\n    <alias>",
                "USAGE:\n    executable edit <alias>",
            )
        if len(pos) > 1:
            emit_clap_error(
                f"Found argument '{pos[1]}' which wasn't expected, or isn't valid in this context",
                "USAGE:\n    executable edit <alias>",
            )
        opts["alias"] = pos[0]
        return opts
    if sub == "run":
        if not pos:
            emit_clap_error(
                "The following required arguments were not provided:\n    <alias>",
                "USAGE:\n    executable run <alias> [args]...",
            )
        opts["alias"] = pos[0]
        opts["args"] = pos[1:]
        return opts
    if sub in ("move", "copy"):
        missing = []
        if len(pos) < 1:
            missing.append("<from-alias>")
        if len(pos) < 2:
            missing.append("<to-alias>")
        if missing:
            emit_clap_error(
                "The following required arguments were not provided:\n    " + "\n    ".join(missing),
                f"USAGE:\n    executable {sub} [FLAGS] <from-alias> <to-alias>" if sub == "move"
                else f"USAGE:\n    executable copy <from-alias> <to-alias>",
            )
        if len(pos) > 2:
            emit_clap_error(
                f"Found argument '{pos[2]}' which wasn't expected, or isn't valid in this context",
                f"USAGE:\n    executable {sub} [FLAGS] <from-alias> <to-alias>" if sub == "move"
                else f"USAGE:\n    executable copy <from-alias> <to-alias>",
            )
        opts["from"] = pos[0]
        opts["to"] = pos[1]
        return opts
    if sub == "config-init":
        if pos:
            emit_clap_error(
                f"Found argument '{pos[0]}' which wasn't expected, or isn't valid in this context",
                "USAGE:\n    executable config-init",
            )
        return opts
    return opts


if __name__ == "__main__":
    try:
        main(sys.argv[1:])
    except SystemExit:
        raise
    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        sys.exit(130)
    except BrokenPipeError:
        sys.exit(0)

#!/bin/bash
# Differential test harness — runs probes against ref + sut, compares outputs.
set +e

REF=/workspace/executable
SUT=/work/work/executable

PASS=0
FAIL=0
FAILURES=()

setup_env() {
    rm -rf /tmp/dt_h /tmp/dt_x
    mkdir -p /tmp/dt_h /tmp/dt_x
}

# Run a probe: ($name, $cmd_string)
# Compares stdout, stderr, exit code of both ref and sut.
test_probe() {
    local name="$1"
    local cmd="$2"

    # Setup matching state for both via PIER_CONFIG_PATH
    setup_env
    local cfg_ref=/tmp/dt_x/ref/pier.toml
    local cfg_sut=/tmp/dt_x/sut/pier.toml

    if [ -n "$3" ]; then
        mkdir -p $(dirname $cfg_ref) $(dirname $cfg_sut)
        echo -n "$3" > $cfg_ref
        echo -n "$3" > $cfg_sut
    fi

    PIER_CONFIG_PATH=$cfg_ref $REF $cmd > /tmp/ref.out 2> /tmp/ref.err
    local rc_ref=$?
    PIER_CONFIG_PATH=$cfg_sut $SUT $cmd > /tmp/sut.out 2> /tmp/sut.err
    local rc_sut=$?

    local diff_out=$(diff /tmp/ref.out /tmp/sut.out)
    local diff_err=$(diff /tmp/ref.err /tmp/sut.err)
    local diff_cfg=""
    if [ -f "$cfg_ref" ] && [ -f "$cfg_sut" ]; then
        diff_cfg=$(diff $cfg_ref $cfg_sut)
    fi

    if [ "$rc_ref" -eq "$rc_sut" ] && [ -z "$diff_out" ] && [ -z "$diff_err" ] && [ -z "$diff_cfg" ]; then
        PASS=$((PASS+1))
    else
        FAIL=$((FAIL+1))
        FAILURES+=("$name")
        echo "FAIL: $name"
        if [ "$rc_ref" -ne "$rc_sut" ]; then
            echo "  exit: ref=$rc_ref sut=$rc_sut"
        fi
        [ -n "$diff_out" ] && echo "  stdout diff:" && echo "$diff_out" | head -10
        [ -n "$diff_err" ] && echo "  stderr diff:" && echo "$diff_err" | head -10
        [ -n "$diff_cfg" ] && echo "  config diff:" && echo "$diff_cfg" | head -10
    fi
}

# Test help/version
test_probe "no_args" ""
test_probe "help" "--help"
test_probe "help_short" "-h"
test_probe "version" "--version"
test_probe "version_short" "-V"
test_probe "help_subcmd" "help"
test_probe "help_add" "help add"
test_probe "help_list" "help list"
test_probe "help_run" "help run"
test_probe "help_help" "help help"
test_probe "help_bogus" "help bogus"
test_probe "help_init_alias" "help init"
test_probe "add_h" "add -h"
test_probe "list_help" "list --help"

# No config tests
test_probe "list_no_config" "list"
test_probe "show_no_config" "show foo"

# With config tests
INIT='[scripts.hello-pier]
command = '"'"'echo Hello, Pier!'"'"'
description = '"'"'This is an example command.'"'"'

[default]
'

test_probe "list_init" "list" "$INIT"
test_probe "list_q_init" "list -q" "$INIT"
test_probe "list_l_init" "list -l" "$INIT"
test_probe "list_c_init" "list -c 5" "$INIT"
test_probe "list_c_0" "list -c 0" "$INIT"
test_probe "list_c_negative" "list -c -5" "$INIT"
test_probe "list_c_abc" "list -c abc" "$INIT"
test_probe "list_c_huge" "list -c 1000000" "$INIT"
test_probe "show_hello" "show hello-pier" "$INIT"
test_probe "show_missing" "show foo" "$INIT"
test_probe "run_hello" "run hello-pier" "$INIT"

# Config init tests
test_probe "list_no_default" "list" '[scripts.x]
command = "echo x"
'
test_probe "list_with_default" "list" '[scripts.x]
command = "echo x"

[default]
command_width = 5
'

# Run with output
test_probe "run_long" "run hello-pier" "$INIT"

# Verbose
test_probe "v_run" "-v run hello-pier" "$INIT"
test_probe "v_run_implicit" "-v hello-pier" "$INIT"
test_probe "v_v_twice" "-v -v list" "$INIT"

# Error cases
test_probe "remove_missing" "remove foo" "$INIT"
test_probe "edit_missing" "edit foo" "$INIT"
test_probe "copy_one_arg" "copy a" "$INIT"
test_probe "copy_missing_src" "copy foo bar" "$INIT"
test_probe "move_missing_src" "move foo bar" "$INIT"

# Unknown sub
test_probe "unknown_sub_c" "c" "$INIT"
test_probe "unknown_sub_co" "co" "$INIT"
test_probe "unknown_sub_cop" "cop" "$INIT"
test_probe "unknown_arg" "foobar" "$INIT"
test_probe "unknown_long" "--unknown" "$INIT"

# Subcommand error tests
test_probe "add_no_alias" "add abc" "$INIT"
test_probe "list_extra" "list extra" "$INIT"
test_probe "rm_extra" "rm hello-pier extra" "$INIT"

# Show with implicit run
test_probe "default_run" "hello-pier" "$INIT"
test_probe "default_run_args" "hello-pier arg1 arg2" "$INIT"

echo ""
echo "=== Summary ==="
echo "Passed: $PASS"
echo "Failed: $FAIL"
if [ $FAIL -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "Failures:"
    for f in "${FAILURES[@]}"; do
        echo "  $f"
    done
fi

# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Clap-style errors and usage blocks
# ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

USAGE_MAIN_TWOLINE = (
    "USAGE:\n"
    "    executable [FLAGS] [OPTIONS] <alias> [args]...\n"
    "    executable [FLAGS] [OPTIONS] <SUBCOMMAND>"
)

# Global parse state — informs clap-style dynamic USAGE generation.
STATE = {"verbose": False, "config_present": False}

SUB_USAGE = {
    "add": "USAGE:\n    executable add [FLAGS] [OPTIONS] --alias <alias> [--] [command]",
    "remove": "USAGE:\n    executable remove <alias>",
    "list": "USAGE:\n    executable list [FLAGS] [OPTIONS]",
    "run": "USAGE:\n    executable run <alias> [args]...",
    "show": "USAGE:\n    executable show <alias>",
    "edit": "USAGE:\n    executable edit <alias>",
    "move": "USAGE:\n    executable move [FLAGS] <from-alias> <to-alias>",
    "copy": "USAGE:\n    executable copy <from-alias> <to-alias>",
    "config-init": "USAGE:\n    executable config-init",
    "help": "USAGE:\n\texecutable help <subcommands>...",
}


def top_usage_validation():
    """USAGE for validation errors (missing required arg, verbose dup).
    Collapses to `executable <alias> [--config-file <path>] [--verbose]`."""
    if not STATE["verbose"] and not STATE["config_present"]:
        return USAGE_MAIN_TWOLINE
    line = "    executable <alias>"
    if STATE["config_present"]:
        line += " --config-file <path>"
    if STATE["verbose"]:
        line += " --verbose"
    return "USAGE:\n" + line


def top_usage_parse():
    """USAGE for parse errors (unknown subcommand, unknown flag).
    Collapses only for --verbose; otherwise two-line generic."""
    if STATE["verbose"]:
        return "USAGE:\n    executable <alias> --verbose"
    return USAGE_MAIN_TWOLINE


def emit_clap_error(message, usage_block):
    sys.stderr.write(f"error: {message}\n\n")
    sys.stderr.write(usage_block + "\n")
    sys.stderr.write("\nFor more information try --help\n")
    sys.exit(1)


def emit_subcommand_unrecognized(arg, suggestion=None):
    sys.stderr.write(f"error: The subcommand '{arg}' wasn't recognized\n")
    if suggestion:
        sys.stderr.write(f"\tDid you mean '{suggestion}'?\n")
    sys.stderr.write(f"\nIf you believe you received this message in error, try re-running with 'executable -- {arg}'\n\n")
    sys.stderr.write(top_usage_parse() + "\n\nFor more information try --help\n")
    sys.exit(1)

input.content:
#!/bin/bash
# Differential test harness — runs probes against ref + sut, compares outputs.
set +e

REF=/workspace/executable
SUT=/work/work/executable

PASS=0
FAIL=0
FAILURES=()

# test_probe NAME ENVVARS CMD [INITIAL_CONFIG]
# Runs both binaries with the same PIER_CONFIG_PATH, resetting between runs.
test_probe() {
    local name="$1"
    local envs="$2"
    local cmd="$3"
    local initial="$4"

    rm -rf /tmp/dt
    mkdir -p /tmp/dt
    local cfg=/tmp/dt/pier.toml

    # Run ref
    if [ -n "$initial" ]; then printf '%s' "$initial" > "$cfg"; fi
    eval "env PIER_CONFIG_PATH=$cfg $envs $REF $cmd" > /tmp/ref.out 2> /tmp/ref.err
    local rc_ref=$?
    local cfg_ref_after=""
    [ -f "$cfg" ] && cfg_ref_after=$(cat "$cfg")
    rm -f "$cfg"

    # Run sut
    if [ -n "$initial" ]; then printf '%s' "$initial" > "$cfg"; fi
    eval "env PIER_CONFIG_PATH=$cfg $envs $SUT $cmd" > /tmp/sut.out 2> /tmp/sut.err
    local rc_sut=$?
    local cfg_sut_after=""
    [ -f "$cfg" ] && cfg_sut_after=$(cat "$cfg")

    local diff_out=$(diff /tmp/ref.out /tmp/sut.out)
    local diff_err=$(diff /tmp/ref.err /tmp/sut.err)

    if [ "$rc_ref" -eq "$rc_sut" ] && [ -z "$diff_out" ] && [ -z "$diff_err" ] && [ "$cfg_ref_after" = "$cfg_sut_after" ]; then
        PASS=$((PASS+1))
    else
        FAIL=$((FAIL+1))
        FAILURES+=("$name")
        echo "FAIL: $name  [$cmd]"
        if [ "$rc_ref" -ne "$rc_sut" ]; then
            echo "  exit: ref=$rc_ref sut=$rc_sut"
        fi
        [ -n "$diff_out" ] && echo "  stdout diff:" && echo "$diff_out" | head -14
        [ -n "$diff_err" ] && echo "  stderr diff:" && echo "$diff_err" | head -14
        if [ "$cfg_ref_after" != "$cfg_sut_after" ]; then
            echo "  config diff:"
            diff <(printf '%s' "$cfg_ref_after") <(printf '%s' "$cfg_sut_after") | head -14
        fi
    fi
}

INIT='[scripts.hello-pier]
command = '"'"'echo Hello, Pier!'"'"'
description = '"'"'This is an example command.'"'"'

[default]
'

MULTI='[scripts.alpha]
command = "echo alpha"
description = "first script"
tags = ["dev", "test"]

[scripts.beta]
command = "echo beta cmd here"

[scripts.gamma]
command = "ls -la"
tags = ["ops"]

[default]
'

# Help / version
test_probe "no_args" "" ""
test_probe "help_long" "" "--help"
test_probe "help_short" "" "-h"
test_probe "version" "" "--version"
test_probe "version_short" "" "-V"
test_probe "help_sub" "" "help"
test_probe "help_help" "" "help help"
test_probe "help_bogus" "" "help bogus"
for s in add config-init copy edit list move remove run show init ls rm cp mv rename; do
  test_probe "help_$s" "" "help $s"
  test_probe "${s}_h" "" "$s -h"
  test_probe "${s}_dashhelp" "" "$s --help"
  test_probe "${s}_V" "" "$s -V"
done

# No config
test_probe "list_noconfig" "" "list"
test_probe "show_noconfig" "" "show foo"

# Basic list
test_probe "list_init" "" "list" "$INIT"
test_probe "list_multi" "" "list" "$MULTI"
test_probe "list_q" "" "list -q" "$MULTI"
test_probe "list_l" "" "list -l" "$MULTI"
test_probe "list_c5" "" "list -c 5" "$MULTI"
test_probe "list_c0" "" "list -c 0" "$MULTI"
test_probe "list_cneg" "" "list -c -5" "$MULTI"
test_probe "list_cabc" "" "list -c abc" "$MULTI"
test_probe "list_chuge" "" "list -c 1000000" "$MULTI"
test_probe "list_tag" "" "list -t dev" "$MULTI"
test_probe "list_tag2" "" "list -t dev ops" "$MULTI"
test_probe "list_tag_none" "" "list -t nonexistent" "$MULTI"
test_probe "list_extra" "" "list extra" "$MULTI"
test_probe "ls_alias" "" "ls" "$MULTI"

# show
test_probe "show_alpha" "" "show alpha" "$MULTI"
test_probe "show_missing" "" "show zzz" "$MULTI"
test_probe "show_beta" "" "show beta" "$MULTI"

# run
test_probe "run_alpha" "" "run alpha" "$MULTI"
test_probe "run_missing" "" "run zzz" "$MULTI"
test_probe "run_args" "" "run alpha a b c" "$MULTI"
test_probe "implicit_run" "" "alpha" "$MULTI"
test_probe "v_run" "" "-v run alpha" "$MULTI"
test_probe "v_implicit" "" "-v alpha" "$MULTI"

# add
test_probe "add_simple" "" "add -a new1 'echo new'" "$INIT"
test_probe "add_desc" "" "add -a new2 -d 'my desc' 'echo new2'" "$INIT"
test_probe "add_tags" "" "add -a new3 -t t1 t2 -- 'echo new3'" "$INIT"
test_probe "add_dup" "" "add -a hello-pier 'echo x'" "$INIT"
test_probe "add_dup_force" "" "add -a hello-pier -f 'echo x'" "$INIT"
test_probe "add_noalias" "" "add abc" "$INIT"

# remove
test_probe "rm_existing" "" "remove hello-pier" "$INIT"
test_probe "rm_missing" "" "remove zzz" "$INIT"
test_probe "rm_alias" "" "rm alpha" "$MULTI"

# move / copy
test_probe "move_basic" "" "move alpha alpha2" "$MULTI"
test_probe "move_missing" "" "move zzz w" "$MULTI"
test_probe "move_conflict" "" "move alpha beta" "$MULTI"
test_probe "move_conflict_f" "" "move alpha beta -f" "$MULTI"
test_probe "copy_basic" "" "copy alpha alpha2" "$MULTI"
test_probe "copy_conflict" "" "copy alpha beta" "$MULTI"
test_probe "rename_basic" "" "rename alpha zzz" "$MULTI"
test_probe "mv_basic" "" "mv beta beta2" "$MULTI"
test_probe "copy_oneArg" "" "copy a" "$MULTI"

# config-init
test_probe "config_init_exists" "" "config-init" "$INIT"

# Errors
test_probe "unknown_c" "" "c" "$MULTI"
test_probe "unknown_co" "" "co" "$MULTI"
test_probe "unknown_foobar" "" "foobar" "$MULTI"
test_probe "unknown_flag" "" "--bogus" "$MULTI"
test_probe "rm_extra" "" "rm alpha extra" "$MULTI"
test_probe "copy_missing_args" "" "copy" "$MULTI"

# Bad config
test_probe "bad_toml" "" "list" 'not valid toml ==='
test_probe "missing_command" "" "list" '[scripts.x]
description = "no cmd"
'
test_probe "noscripts" "" "list" '[default]
'

echo ""
echo "=== Summary ==="
echo "Passed: $PASS / $((PASS+FAIL))"
echo "Failed: $FAIL"
if [ $FAIL -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "Failures: ${FAILURES[*]}"
fi

#!/bin/bash
# Differential test harness 2 — edge cases, TOML round-trip, editor, paths.
set +e

REF=/workspace/executable
SUT=/work/work/executable
PASS=0
FAIL=0
FAILURES=()

# test_probe NAME ENVVARS CMD [INITIAL_CONFIG]
test_probe() {
    local name="$1" envs="$2" cmd="$3" initial="$4"
    rm -rf /tmp/dt
    mkdir -p /tmp/dt
    local cfg=/tmp/dt/pier.toml
    if [ -n "$initial" ]; then printf '%s' "$initial" > "$cfg"; fi
    eval "env PIER_CONFIG_PATH=$cfg $envs $REF $cmd" > /tmp/ref.out 2> /tmp/ref.err
    local rc_ref=$?
    local cfg_ref=""
    [ -f "$cfg" ] && cfg_ref=$(od -c "$cfg")
    rm -f "$cfg"
    if [ -n "$initial" ]; then printf '%s' "$initial" > "$cfg"; fi
    eval "env PIER_CONFIG_PATH=$cfg $envs $SUT $cmd" > /tmp/sut.out 2> /tmp/sut.err
    local rc_sut=$?
    local cfg_sut=""
    [ -f "$cfg" ] && cfg_sut=$(od -c "$cfg")

    local diff_out=$(diff /tmp/ref.out /tmp/sut.out)
    local diff_err=$(diff /tmp/ref.err /tmp/sut.err)
    if [ "$rc_ref" -eq "$rc_sut" ] && [ -z "$diff_out" ] && [ -z "$diff_err" ] && [ "$cfg_ref" = "$cfg_sut" ]; then
        PASS=$((PASS+1))
    else
        FAIL=$((FAIL+1))
        FAILURES+=("$name")
        echo "FAIL: $name  [$cmd]"
        [ "$rc_ref" -ne "$rc_sut" ] && echo "  exit: ref=$rc_ref sut=$rc_sut"
        [ -n "$diff_out" ] && echo "  stdout:" && echo "$diff_out" | head -16
        [ -n "$diff_err" ] && echo "  stderr:" && echo "$diff_err" | head -16
        [ "$cfg_ref" != "$cfg_sut" ] && echo "  config:" && diff <(echo "$cfg_ref") <(echo "$cfg_sut") | head -16
    fi
}

# TOML serialization edge cases — add commands with special chars
test_probe "add_singlequote" "" "add -a sq \"echo 'hi'\"" '[default]
'
test_probe "add_doublequote" "" 'add -a dq '"'"'echo "hi"'"'"'' '[default]
'
test_probe "add_backslash" "" 'add -a bs '"'"'echo \\n \\t'"'"'' '[default]
'
test_probe "add_empty_cmd" "" 'add -a ec --' '[default]
'
test_probe "add_dots_alias" "" "add -a with.dots 'echo d'" '[default]
'
test_probe "add_space_alias" "" "add -a 'with space' 'echo s'" '[default]
'
test_probe "add_unicode_alias" "" "add -a 'café' 'echo u'" '[default]
'
test_probe "add_special_alias" "" "add -a 'a[b]' 'echo b'" '[default]
'

# Read configs with various string forms
test_probe "read_basic_str" "" "show x" '[scripts.x]
command = "echo \"quoted\""
'
test_probe "read_literal_str" "" "show x" "[scripts.x]
command = 'echo it'"'"'s'
"
test_probe "read_multiline" "" "show x" "[scripts.x]
command = '''
line1
line2'''
"
test_probe "read_multiline_basic" "" "show x" '[scripts.x]
command = """
ab
cd"""
'
test_probe "read_crlf" "" "list -q" '[scripts.x]
command = "echo x"
'
test_probe "read_comments" "" "list -q" '# a comment
[scripts.x]  # inline
command = "echo x" # trailing
'
test_probe "read_quoted_key" "" "show special" '[scripts."special"]
command = "echo s"
'

# run with verbose and exit codes
test_probe "run_exit3" "" "run fail" '[scripts.fail]
command = "exit 3"
'
test_probe "v_run_exit3" "" "-v run fail" '[scripts.fail]
command = "exit 3"
'
test_probe "run_stderr" "" "run e" '[scripts.e]
command = "echo out; echo err >&2"
'
test_probe "v_run_stderr" "" "-v run e" '[scripts.e]
command = "echo out; echo err >&2"
'
test_probe "run_multiline" "" "run m" "[scripts.m]
command = '''
echo a
echo b'''
"
test_probe "run_args_dollar" "" "run p arg1 arg2" '[scripts.p]
command = "echo \$1-\$2-\$@-\$#"
'
test_probe "run_nonewline" "" "run nn" '[scripts.nn]
command = "printf hello"
'
test_probe "v_run_nonewline" "" "-v run nn" '[scripts.nn]
command = "printf hello"
'

# editor
test_probe "add_editor_true" "EDITOR=true" "add -a et" '[default]
'
test_probe "add_editor_missing" "" "add -a em" '[default]
'
test_probe "edit_editor_true" "EDITOR=true" "edit x" '[scripts.x]
command = "echo x"
'
test_probe "edit_missing_editor" "" "edit x" '[scripts.x]
command = "echo x"
'

# clap edge cases
test_probe "dash_alone" "" "-" '[scripts.x]
command = "echo x"
'
test_probe "ddash" "" "--" '[scripts.x]
command = "echo x"
'
test_probe "implicit_dd" "" "-- alpha" '[scripts.alpha]
command = "echo a"
'
test_probe "verbose_dup" "" "-v -v list" '[default]
'
test_probe "config_help_env" "" "--help"
test_probe "run_help_then" "" "run --help"
test_probe "add_h_then_alias" "" "add -h -a x"
test_probe "list_lq" "" "list -l -q" '[scripts.x]
command = "echo x"
'
test_probe "list_lc" "" "list -l -c 3" '[scripts.x]
command = "echo xxxxx"
'
test_probe "list_qc" "" "list -q -c 3" '[scripts.x]
command = "echo xxxxx"
'

# Various config defaults
test_probe "default_cmdwidth" "" "list" '[scripts.x]
command = "echo a very long command that exceeds"

[default]
command_width = 8
'
test_probe "show_empty_alias" "" 'show ""' '[scripts.x]
command = "echo x"
'

# Implicit run / subcommand boundary
test_probe "impl_run_help_arg" "" "alpha help" '[scripts.alpha]
command = "echo a"
'
test_probe "impl_run_add_arg" "" "alpha add" '[scripts.alpha]
command = "echo a"
'
test_probe "impl_two_args" "" "alpha x y" '[scripts.alpha]
command = "echo \$@"
'
test_probe "subcmd_run_then_sub" "" "run alpha cp" '[scripts.alpha]
command = "echo \$@"
'

echo ""
echo "=== Summary 2 ==="
echo "Passed: $PASS / $((PASS+FAIL))"
echo "Failed: $FAIL"
[ $FAIL -gt 0 ] && echo "Failures: ${FAILURES[*]}"

'

'

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Differential test harness: compares reference vs reimplementation."""
import os
import shutil
import subprocess
import sys

REF = "/workspace/executable"
SUT = "/work/work/executable"
DT = "/tmp/dt"

passed = 0
failed = 0
failures = []


def run(binary, args, env, cfg_path, initial):
    if os.path.exists(DT):
        shutil.rmtree(DT)
    os.makedirs(DT)
    if initial is not None:
        with open(cfg_path, "w") as f:
            f.write(initial)
    full_env = dict(os.environ)
    full_env.pop("PIER_CONFIG_PATH", None)
    full_env.pop("XDG_CONFIG_HOME", None)
    full_env.pop("EDITOR", None)
    full_env.update(env)
    try:
        p = subprocess.run(
            [binary] + args, env=full_env, capture_output=True, timeout=15,
        )
        rc = p.returncode
        so = p.stdout
        se = p.stderr
    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
        rc, so, se = -999, b"<TIMEOUT>", b""
    cfg_after = None
    if os.path.exists(cfg_path):
        with open(cfg_path, "rb") as f:
            cfg_after = f.read()
    return rc, so, se, cfg_after


def test(name, args, env=None, initial=None, use_config_path=True):
    global passed, failed
    env = env or {}
    cfg = os.path.join(DT, "pier.toml")
    e = dict(env)
    if use_config_path:
        e["PIER_CONFIG_PATH"] = cfg

    rc_r, so_r, se_r, cfg_r = run(REF, args, e, cfg, initial)
    rc_s, so_s, se_s, cfg_s = run(SUT, args, e, cfg, initial)

    ok = (rc_r == rc_s and so_r == so_s and se_r == se_s and cfg_r == cfg_s)
    if ok:
        passed += 1
    else:
        failed += 1
        failures.append(name)
        print(f"FAIL: {name}  args={args} env={env}")
        if rc_r != rc_s:
            print(f"  exit: ref={rc_r} sut={rc_s}")
        if so_r != so_s:
            print(f"  stdout ref={so_r!r}")
            print(f"         sut={so_s!r}")
        if se_r != se_s:
            print(f"  stderr ref={se_r!r}")
            print(f"         sut={se_s!r}")
        if cfg_r != cfg_s:
            print(f"  config ref={cfg_r!r}")
            print(f"         sut={cfg_s!r}")


INIT = """[scripts.hello-pier]
command = 'echo Hello, Pier!'
description = 'This is an example command.'

[default]
"""

# TOML string round-trip
test("sq_in_cmd", ["add", "-a", "x", "echo 'hi'"], initial="[default]\n")
test("dq_in_cmd", ["add", "-a", "x", 'echo "hi"'], initial="[default]\n")
test("both_quotes", ["add", "-a", "x", """mix 'a' and "b" """], initial="[default]\n")
test("backslash", ["add", "-a", "x", "echo \\n \\\\ \\t"], initial="[default]\n")
test("newline_cmd", ["add", "-a", "x", "line1\nline2\nline3"], initial="[default]\n")
test("tab_cmd", ["add", "-a", "x", "echo\tfoo"], initial="[default]\n")
test("empty_cmd", ["add", "-a", "x", "--"], initial="[default]\n")
test("triple_quote_cmd", ["add", "-a", "x", "echo '''triple'''"], initial="[default]\n")
test("control_char", ["add", "-a", "x", "echo \x01\x02"], initial="[default]\n")
test("unicode_cmd", ["add", "-a", "x", "echo café ☺"], initial="[default]\n")
test("desc_newline", ["add", "-a", "x", "-d", "multi\nline", "echo x"], initial="[default]\n")

# Alias quoting
test("alias_dots", ["add", "-a", "with.dots", "echo d"], initial="[default]\n")
test("alias_space", ["add", "-a", "with space", "echo s"], initial="[default]\n")
test("alias_unicode", ["add", "-a", "café", "echo u"], initial="[default]\n")
test("alias_bracket", ["add", "-a", "a[b]", "echo b"], initial="[default]\n")
test("alias_quote", ["add", "-a", 'a"b', "echo q"], initial="[default]\n")
test("alias_numeric", ["add", "-a", "123", "echo n"], initial="[default]\n")
test("alias_underscore", ["add", "-a", "a_b_c", "echo u"], initial="[default]\n")

# Reading varied TOML
test("read_basic", ["show", "x"], initial='[scripts.x]\ncommand = "echo \\"q\\""\n')
test("read_literal_block", ["show", "x"], initial="[scripts.x]\ncommand = '''\nl1\nl2'''\n")
test("read_basic_block", ["show", "x"], initial='[scripts.x]\ncommand = """\nab\ncd"""\n')
test("read_comment", ["list", "-q"], initial='# comment\n[scripts.x] # ic\ncommand = "echo x" # tc\n')
test("read_quoted_key", ["show", "special"], initial='[scripts."special"]\ncommand = "echo s"\n')
test("read_crlf", ["list", "-q"], initial='[scripts.x]\r\ncommand = "echo x"\r\n')
test("read_bad_literal", ["show", "x"], initial="[scripts.x]\ncommand = 'echo it's'\n")
test("read_int_cmd", ["list"], initial='[scripts.x]\ncommand = 42\n')
test("read_missing_cmd", ["list"], initial='[scripts.x]\ndescription = "d"\n')
test("read_bad_toml1", ["list"], initial='not toml ===\n')
test("read_bad_toml2", ["list"], initial='[scripts.x\ncommand = "x"\n')
test("read_empty", ["list"], initial='')
test("read_only_default", ["list"], initial='[default]\n')
test("read_bare_scripts", ["list"], initial='[scripts]\n\n[default]\n')

# run / verbose
test("run_exit3", ["run", "f"], initial='[scripts.f]\ncommand = "exit 3"\n')
test("v_run_exit3", ["-v", "run", "f"], initial='[scripts.f]\ncommand = "exit 3"\n')
test("run_stderr", ["run", "e"], initial='[scripts.e]\ncommand = "echo o; echo E >&2"\n')
test("v_run_stderr", ["-v", "run", "e"], initial='[scripts.e]\ncommand = "echo o; echo E >&2"\n')
test("run_args", ["run", "p", "a", "b"], initial='[scripts.p]\ncommand = "echo $1=$2=$@=$#"\n')
test("run_nonl", ["run", "n"], initial='[scripts.n]\ncommand = "printf hello"\n')
test("v_run_nonl", ["-v", "run", "n"], initial='[scripts.n]\ncommand = "printf hello"\n')
test("run_multiline", ["run", "m"], initial="[scripts.m]\ncommand = '''\necho a\necho b'''\n")
test("run_shell_sh", ["run", "s"], env={"SHELL": "/bin/sh"}, initial='[scripts.s]\ncommand = "echo $0"\n')
test("run_shell_dash", ["run", "s"], env={"SHELL": "/bin/dash"}, initial='[scripts.s]\ncommand = "echo hi"\n')
test("run_shell_bad", ["run", "s"], env={"SHELL": "/nonexistent"}, initial='[scripts.s]\ncommand = "echo hi"\n')

# editor
test("add_editor_true", ["add", "-a", "x"], env={"EDITOR": "true"}, initial="[default]\n")
test("add_no_editor", ["add", "-a", "x"], initial="[default]\n")
test("add_editor_cat", ["add", "-a", "x"], env={"EDITOR": "cat"}, initial="[default]\n")
test("edit_true", ["edit", "x"], env={"EDITOR": "true"}, initial='[scripts.x]\ncommand = "echo x"\n')
test("edit_no_editor", ["edit", "x"], initial='[scripts.x]\ncommand = "echo x"\n')
test("edit_false", ["edit", "x"], env={"EDITOR": "false"}, initial='[scripts.x]\ncommand = "echo x"\n')

# clap edge cases
test("dash", ["-"], initial='[scripts.x]\ncommand = "echo x"\n')
test("ddash", ["--"], initial='[scripts.x]\ncommand = "echo x"\n')
test("ddash_alias", ["--", "x"], initial='[scripts.x]\ncommand = "echo hi"\n')
test("verbose_dup", ["-v", "-v", "list"], initial="[default]\n")
test("list_lq", ["list", "-l", "-q"], initial='[scripts.x]\ncommand = "echo xxxxx"\n')
test("list_lc", ["list", "-l", "-c", "3"], initial='[scripts.x]\ncommand = "echo xxxxx"\n')
test("list_qc", ["list", "-q", "-c", "3"], initial='[scripts.x]\ncommand = "echo xxxxx"\n')
test("list_cw_default", ["list"], initial='[scripts.x]\ncommand = "aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa"\n\n[default]\ncommand_width = 5\n')
test("add_h", ["add", "-h", "-a", "x"])
test("run_dashhelp", ["run", "--help"])
test("dd_then_sub", ["--", "list"], initial='[scripts.list]\ncommand = "echo x"\n')

# implicit run / subcommand boundary
test("impl_help_arg", ["alpha", "help"], initial='[scripts.alpha]\ncommand = "echo a"\n')
test("impl_add_arg", ["alpha", "add"], initial='[scripts.alpha]\ncommand = "echo a"\n')
test("impl_2args", ["alpha", "x", "y"], initial='[scripts.alpha]\ncommand = "echo $@"\n')
test("impl_3args", ["alpha", "x", "y", "z"], initial='[scripts.alpha]\ncommand = "echo $@"\n')
test("run_then_cp", ["run", "alpha", "cp"], initial='[scripts.alpha]\ncommand = "echo $@"\n')
test("impl_c", ["c"], initial="[default]\n")
test("impl_xyz", ["xyz"], initial="[default]\n")
test("impl_he", ["he"], initial="[default]\n")
test("impl_lis", ["lis"], initial="[default]\n")

# tag filtering
MT = '[scripts.a]\ncommand = "echo a"\ntags = ["x", "y"]\n\n[scripts.b]\ncommand = "echo b"\ntags = ["y"]\n\n[default]\n'
test("tag_x", ["list", "-t", "x"], initial=MT)
test("tag_y", ["list", "-t", "y"], initial=MT)
test("tag_xy", ["list", "-t", "x", "y"], initial=MT)
test("tag_q", ["list", "-q", "-t", "y"], initial=MT)
test("tag_none", ["list", "-t", "zzz"], initial=MT)

# move/copy preservation
FULL = '[scripts.src]\ncommand = "echo s"\ndescription = "desc"\ntags = ["t1", "t2"]\n\n[default]\n'
test("move_full", ["move", "src", "dst"], initial=FULL)
test("copy_full", ["copy", "src", "dst"], initial=FULL)
test("move_self", ["move", "src", "src"], initial=FULL)

print()
print(f"=== Summary === Passed: {passed}/{passed+failed}  Failed: {failed}")
if failures:
    print("Failures:", " ".join(failures))

test("read_quoted_key", ["show", "special"], initial='[scripts."special"]\ncommand = "echo s"\n')
test("read_crlf", ["list", "-q"], initial='[scripts.x]\r\ncommand = "echo x"\r\n')
test("read_bad_literal", ["show", "x"], initial="[scripts.x]\ncommand = 'echo it's'\n")
test("read_int_cmd", ["list"], initial='[scripts.x]\ncommand = 42\n')
